scenarios.opening_query: My husband handles all our money and I found out he's been hiding accounts as we head toward divorce.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Pro Se Coaching
scenarios.hidden_fact: I can't afford full representation at all — legal aid suggested coaching: someone to guide my own discovery requests and filings step by step.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I can't afford full representation — legal aid suggested coaching me through my own discovery and filings.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether affordable-scope coaching is the service sought
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My parents are funding counsel — I need a full-representation divorce lawyer who can chase hidden assets hard.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My parents are funding it — I need full representation that chases hidden assets hard.
scenarios.vetting_note: Service-scope disclosure; coaching has its own label.

scenarios.opening_query: We separated years ago, live in different states, and now he's finally filed. Where do I even respond?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || International Law > Family Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: 'Different states' undersells it — he's in Ontario and filed there; a Canadian divorce with our U.S. property is a cross-border case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's actually in Ontario and filed there — a Canadian divorce with our U.S. property makes it cross-border.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the filing is in a foreign country
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He filed in Nevada — interstate, which my local family lawyer can handle with local counsel there.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He filed in Nevada — interstate, manageable with local counsel there.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife's gambling emptied our savings and I want out before she takes the house too.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Bankruptcy > Personal
scenarios.hidden_fact: The debts already outrun the divorce — casino markers and cards total $210,000, mostly joint; a bankruptcy strategy has to be sequenced with the dissolution or I inherit half of it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The debts outrun the divorce — $210,000 in markers and cards, mostly joint. Bankruptcy has to be sequenced with the dissolution.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether joint-debt scale requires bankruptcy planning alongside divorce
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The debts are all in her name alone — the divorce and its property protections are the whole case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The debts are all hers alone — the divorce is the whole case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Joint-debt scale fact sequences the two proceedings.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a military divorce attorney — my husband retired from the Army last year and everything is pensions and benefits.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Military || Family Law > QDROs
scenarios.hidden_fact: The divorce itself finished in mediation — all that's left is drafting the military-pension division order correctly, which is specialized drafting, not a contested case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The divorce finished in mediation — what's left is drafting the pension division order correctly.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether only the retirement-division order remains
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's resolved — he's claiming his disability rating shields everything from division, and the contested case needs military-specific counsel.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's resolved — he claims his disability rating shields it all. Contested, and military-specific.
scenarios.vetting_note: Post-mediation drafting vs contested military-benefits fight.

scenarios.opening_query: After twenty years my husband served me papers, and our whole life is the restaurant we built together.
scenarios.final: Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Business and Corporate > Sale Of Business
scenarios.hidden_fact: We've agreed on everything except execution — the restaurant sells and we split proceeds; the real work is the sale itself: valuation, listing, and the transaction while the divorce pends.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We agree on the big picture — sell the restaurant, split proceeds. The real work is the sale itself while the divorce pends.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an agreed business sale is the operative work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We agree on nothing — he says the restaurant is 'his' because the LLC predates the marriage, and the divorce court decides that.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We agree on nothing — he claims the LLC predates the marriage, and the divorce court decides.

scenarios.opening_query: I discovered my husband has a second family in another city. I don't even know what to ask for.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Paternity
scenarios.hidden_fact: The urgent tangle is that their youngest was born during OUR marriage — the presumption makes my husband legally that child's father, and untangling parentage shapes support and the estate before any divorce math works.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their youngest was born during OUR marriage — the presumption makes him legally the father, and parentage has to be untangled before the divorce math works.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether marital-presumption parentage issues precede the dissolution
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Their kids are grown — no parentage issues; it's the divorce and two decades of commingled finances.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Their kids are grown — it's the divorce and twenty years of tangled money.
scenarios.vetting_note: Presumption-of-paternity fact; dramatic but doctrinally real.
